text: Working with APIs is a fundamental skill for modern developers. APIs allow different software applications to communicate with each other. When you make an HTTP request to an API, it usually returns data in JSON format, which is easy for both humans and machines to read. However, before accessing many APIs, you need authentication—often an API key or token—to prove your identity. Once connected, you must respect rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period. Finally, always implement error handling to manage problems like network failures or incorrect requests. This keeps your application stable and reliable.
